title: FATALERROR
text: FATALERROR Operation activated by the SAX parser when it issues a non-recoverable error. Explain this code operation FATALERROR params string List: in endparams Implement your code here end; FATALERROR operation Copy Parameters List Associated list identifying the location and nature of the error. The identifiers can be: LINE —line number within the XML document where the error occurred COLUMN —column number within the XML document where the error occurred MESSAGE —error message issued by the parser. If provided by the SAX parser, the following may also be in the list: PUBLICID —public identifier of an XML entity SYSTEMID —local identifier of an XML entity Description After issuing a fatal error, no other operation of the handler instance will be activated. Instead, PARSE and PARSEFILE return with a negative value. If FATALERROR returns a negative value, this value is returned by PARSE and PARSEFILE . Otherwise, they return error -151, <UACTERR_EXCEPTION_THROWN> . Related Links PARSE Parses a specified XML stream. PARSEFILE Parses an XML document in the specified file. Was this topic helpful?
